mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45% over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates stagflation expectations that compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235 and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over 100 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which correlates with rising corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month payroll employment decline exceeding 500,000 signals severe economic disruption that triggers immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 500k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 70% of months following a PAYEMS drop greater than 500,000, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth paired with stable consumer price inflation indicates a healthy economic expansion that supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL signal yields a negative average forward return over 60 days when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL remains below 240.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative drift when INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously, with p-value greater than 0.10 over 50 historical instances.
